steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage opens with a thesis — "initiatives that rely on natural features or processes can help address such challenges" — and then gives one example: restoring logjams to help blueback salmon recover. The correct answer must capture that thesis, not just the example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A partnership between the Quinault Indian Nation and Wild Salmon Center shows the importance of collaborative approaches to preserving biodiversity.: The passage mentions the partnership, but partnership/collaboration is not what the passage argues. The logjam method matters, not who coordinated it. ✗
  • Choice B — As indicated by a recent project, logjams help the blueback salmon thrive and reproduce.: This is a supporting detail, not the main idea. The passage uses logjams to illustrate a broader principle. ✗
  • Choice C — Scientists now realize that nature-based conservation methods offer better long-term solutions to environmental issues than methods that are not nature-based do.: The passage never compares nature-based and non-nature-based methods, and attributes no "new realization" to scientists. ✗
  • Choice D — Nature-based approaches can be effective ways to achieve conservation goals.: This restates the opening thesis directly. The logjam example is offered as proof. ✓
D is correct.
